---
title: Community Health Policy Boundaries
description: Separates licensing, conduct, contribution, support, security, and legal terms during community health generation.
---

## Policy Responsibilities

| Artifact | Governs | Must not imply |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `LICENSE` | Rights to use, copy, modify, and distribute repository content | Community behavior, support, security response, or hosted-service terms |
| `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` | Behavior, scope, reporting, moderation, and enforcement | Product warranty, security support, or contribution acceptance |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | Contribution workflow, review evidence, scope, and contributor responsibilities | Guaranteed acceptance, employment, ownership transfer, or legal advice |
| `SECURITY.md` | Private vulnerability reporting and disclosure coordination | Supported-product warranty, SLA, bounty, or guaranteed remediation |
| `SUPPORT.md` | Where and how users request help | Production support, response-time commitment, or consulting relationship |
| Participation terms | Conditions for using hosted labs or participating in a program | Legal approval before qualified review |

## Contributor Covenant

Fetch the current stable Markdown source from the official Contributor Covenant
site during generation. Record the version and source URL. Replace every
official template note, especially reporting and enforcement guidance, before
validation passes. Preserve its visible attribution and applicable license.

Do not silently upgrade an existing adopted code of conduct. Present the
version delta and obtain approval.

## Legal Review Gate

The skill may produce:

* A requirements brief identifying intended clauses, open questions, source
  policies, operational owners, and legal-review needs
* A visibly marked non-operative draft when explicitly requested

The skill must not state that generated terms are adopted, binding, complete,
or legally sufficient. Governing law, venue, eligibility, privacy commitments,
data retention, indemnity, and dispute language require qualified legal input.

## Privacy And Security

* Conduct and vulnerability reports require private channels.
* Public issue forms must warn users not to submit secrets, credentials,
  personal data, confidential information, or vulnerability details.
* Support guidance must route security and conduct reports away from public channels.
* Cloud and third-party service guidance must identify that users control their
  own accounts, resources, access, and charges unless an approved program says otherwise.

## Contribution Agreements

Do not introduce DCO sign-off, a CLA, copyright assignment, patent terms, or AI
disclosure requirements unless maintainers explicitly select them and the
repository has an operational process to enforce them.
